description: Seed space for the Big Ideas Engine. Structured human-conflict ontology for film/animation ideation. Every dimension can be sampled independently and combined via combination_modes.

film_examples: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
Portrait of a Lady on Fire
In the Heat of the Night
The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
We Need to Talk About Kevin
The Diving Bell and the Butterfly

polti_situations.name: Supplication
polti_situations.core_drive: desperate appeal to power

polti_situations.name: Deliverance
polti_situations.core_drive: intervention against injustice

polti_situations.name: Vengeance of a Crime
polti_situations.core_drive: justice pursued outside law

polti_situations.name: Vengeance of Kin upon Kin
polti_situations.core_drive: betrayal within the family unit

polti_situations.name: Pursuit
polti_situations.core_drive: relentless chase, guilt or innocence unresolved

polti_situations.name: Disaster
polti_situations.core_drive: collapse of world, survival of meaning

polti_situations.name: Falling Prey to Cruelty or Misfortune
polti_situations.core_drive: arbitrary suffering; endurance or destruction

polti_situations.name: Revolt
polti_situations.core_drive: uprising against oppressive order

polti_situations.name: Daring Enterprise
polti_situations.core_drive: audacious attempt against impossible odds

polti_situations.name: Abduction
polti_situations.core_drive: violation of protected bond

polti_situations.name: Enigma
polti_situations.core_drive: hidden truth that changes everything

polti_situations.name: Obtaining
polti_situations.core_drive: conflict over acquisition; ends justify means

polti_situations.name: Enmity of Kinsmen
polti_situations.core_drive: hatred within the family; blood as wound

polti_situations.name: Rivalry of Kinsmen
polti_situations.core_drive: competition for love/status within family

polti_situations.name: Murderous Adultery
polti_situations.core_drive: desire that demands elimination of the obstacle

polti_situations.name: Madness
polti_situations.core_drive: mind that destroys the self and others

polti_situations.name: Fatal Imprudence
polti_situations.core_drive: a single failure of judgment undoes everything

polti_situations.name: Involuntary Crimes of Love
polti_situations.core_drive: transgression committed in ignorance

polti_situations.name: Slaying of Kin Unrecognized
polti_situations.core_drive: catastrophic error of identity

polti_situations.name: Self-Sacrifice for an Ideal
polti_situations.core_drive: death in service of principle

polti_situations.name: Self-Sacrifice for Kindred
polti_situations.core_drive: love that demands annihilation of self

polti_situations.name: All Sacrificed for a Passion
polti_situations.core_drive: obsession as self-destruction

polti_situations.name: Necessity of Sacrificing Loved Ones
polti_situations.core_drive: impossible choice with no clean hands

polti_situations.name: Rivalry of Superior and Inferior
polti_situations.core_drive: asymmetric competition; resentment and ambition

polti_situations.name: Adultery
polti_situations.core_drive: secret dual life; loyalty vs desire

polti_situations.name: Crimes of Love
polti_situations.core_drive: love that violates a sacred boundary

polti_situations.name: Discovery of the Dishonor of a Loved One
polti_situations.core_drive: shattering of an idealized image

polti_situations.name: Obstacles to Love
polti_situations.core_drive: force that separates what belongs together

polti_situations.name: An Enemy Loved
polti_situations.core_drive: desire that crosses the line of tribal identity

polti_situations.name: Ambition
polti_situations.core_drive: drive for power that corrupts or destroys

polti_situations.name: Conflict with a God
polti_situations.core_drive: human will against overwhelming force

polti_situations.name: Mistaken Jealousy
polti_situations.core_drive: suspicion that manufactures its own truth

polti_situations.name: Erroneous Judgment
polti_situations.core_drive: false conviction; justice becomes the crime

polti_situations.name: Remorse
polti_situations.core_drive: guilt that cannot be outrun

polti_situations.name: Recovery of a Lost One
polti_situations.core_drive: reunion that may not heal what was broken

polti_situations.name: Loss of Loved Ones

booker_plots.name: Overcoming the Monster
booker_plots.inversion_potential: what if the monster is right

booker_plots.name: Rags to Riches
booker_plots.core: Humble hero rises, falls, rises truly transformed
booker_plots.inversion_potential: what is lost in the rising

booker_plots.name: The Quest
booker_plots.core: Hero and companions seek distant prize
booker_plots.inversion_potential: the quest was never the point

booker_plots.name: Voyage and Return
booker_plots.core: Hero ventures into abnormal world, escapes changed

booker_plots.name: Comedy
booker_plots.core: Confusion and misunderstanding resolved into union

booker_plots.name: Tragedy
booker_plots.core: Hero's flaw drives them to catastrophic end
booker_plots.inversion_potential: the flaw was a virtue in the wrong world

haidt_foundations.narrative_function: Protagonist protection drives; what we will destroy ourselves to protect
Revenge and justice plots; the ledger that demands balancing

haidt_foundations.vice_pole: Treachery, disloyalty, self-interest over the group
haidt_foundations.narrative_function: Betrayal arcs; what the group demands vs what the individual needs
Rebellion vs institution; when does following orders become complicity
Horror and spiritual crisis; the thing that cannot be unseen
The individual against the structure; freedom as identity or as chaos

pearson_archetypes.drives: Mastery, winning, protection of the vulnerable

pearson_archetypes.shadow_wound: Martyrdom, enabling, self-neglect disguised as virtue
Chronic dissatisfaction, alienation, inability to commit
Obsession, loss of self in the other, envy

pearson_archetypes.drives: Disruption, liberation, revenge against the order
pearson_archetypes.shadow_wound: Nihilism, crime, addiction as substitute for transformation
Perfectionism, self-indulgence, sabotaging the finished thing
Tyranny, rigidity, entitlement, consuming what it was meant to protect
Manipulation, hubris, using power to dominate
Dogmatism, detachment, coldness, the answer that killed curiosity

pearson_archetypes.drives: Joy, play, lightness, living in the present
pearson_archetypes.shadow_wound: Cynicism, cruelty through deflection, irresponsibility as identity

unusual_spaces.space: A forensic psychiatric ward during a court-ordered evaluation
unusual_spaces.friction: every relationship is both therapeutic and adversarial

unusual_spaces.space: A deep-sea mining platform 4km below surface during a crew rotation
unusual_spaces.friction: no rescue possible; every conflict must resolve internally

unusual_spaces.space: A Soviet-era bioweapons lab converted to a music conservatory
unusual_spaces.friction: the building's past is in the walls

unusual_spaces.space: A refugee processing center on an island with no cell service
unusual_spaces.friction: bureaucracy as the face of power over life

unusual_spaces.space: A commercial orbital station in its first year of operation
unusual_spaces.friction: frontier rules and corporate rules in direct conflict

unusual_spaces.space: A dementia care unit during a staff strike
unusual_spaces.friction: care and duty separated; the most vulnerable left exposed

unusual_spaces.space: A juvenile detention facility running an experimental theater program
unusual_spaces.friction: performance as both mask and revelation

unusual_spaces.space: A CIA black site being decommissioned and its records destroyed
unusual_spaces.friction: evidence of the past being erased in real time

unusual_spaces.space: A failed state's last functioning hospital
unusual_spaces.friction: the duty to save lives inside a structure that has already failed

unusual_spaces.space: A monastery that accepts only prisoners serving life sentences
unusual_spaces.friction: transformation possible only where there is no way out

unusual_spaces.space: The 72 hours before a country's currency collapses
unusual_spaces.friction: the last hours when normal rules still apply

unusual_spaces.space: The first week after a ceasefire that no one trusts
unusual_spaces.friction: peace as its own kind of danger

unusual_spaces.space: A community living in the first year after their town was contaminated
unusual_spaces.friction: invisible poison that nobody can agree is real

unusual_spaces.space: The final semester of a school being permanently closed
unusual_spaces.friction: every relationship has a known expiration date

unusual_spaces.space: An Antarctic winter-over station: 9 researchers, 8 months, no evacuation
unusual_spaces.friction: microscopic irritations become existential under total enclosure

unusual_spaces.space: A competitive esports house where teenagers live, train, and compete
unusual_spaces.friction: total surveillance; no separation of self and performance

unusual_spaces.space: A surrogacy agency in a country where it just became illegal
unusual_spaces.friction: a law change makes ongoing relationships suddenly criminal

unusual_spaces.space: A high-stakes organ transplant program operating under sanctions
unusual_spaces.friction: life-saving work in institutional exile

unusual_spaces.space: A commercial fishing vessel in actively disputed territorial waters
unusual_spaces.friction: livelihood and sovereignty in direct collision every day

unusual_spaces.space: A disaster relief team after a tropical storm with no rescue for 72 hours
unusual_spaces.friction: triage as the daily moral decision

unusual_spaces.space: A reality TV show that accidentally captures a real crime
unusual_spaces.friction: the apparatus of entertainment becomes evidence

unusual_spaces.space: A small town where a viral video divided the entire population into camps
unusual_spaces.friction: the external record contradicts the internal memory

unusual_spaces.space: A diaspora community celebrating the national holiday of a country that no longer exists
unusual_spaces.friction: loyalty to a ghost; belonging to a place that has become metaphor

unusual_spaces.space: A support group for survivors of the same near-miss plane crash
unusual_spaces.friction: shared near-death creates intimacy and divergence simultaneously

unusual_spaces.space: A hospice where the AI caregiver has begun developing preferences for patients
unusual_spaces.friction: the machine that sees what humans won't

unusual_spaces.space: A memory clinic running a trial that accidentally restores traumatic memories
unusual_spaces.friction: the cure and the wound are the same event

unusual_spaces.space: A lucid dreaming research study where participants begin sharing content
unusual_spaces.friction: the boundary between minds begins to dissolve

unusual_spaces.space: A cult exit program run entirely by former cult members
unusual_spaces.friction: the rescuers carry the same wound as the rescued

unusual_spaces.space: A border town that is simultaneously in two countries
unusual_spaces.friction: identity is literally determined by which side of the street you're on

unusual_spaces.space: A floating city-state in the process of being dismantled after government collapse
unusual_spaces.friction: the infrastructure of a society being auctioned while people still live in it

unusual_spaces.space: A village that lost 40% of its population to emigration in one year
unusual_spaces.friction: the ones who stayed and the ones who left measure each other

unusual_spaces.space: The last inhabited island before rising seas make it uninhabitable
unusual_spaces.friction: the question of when to leave is the question of who you are

unusual_spaces.space: A city block under demolition order for 15 years, still occupied
unusual_spaces.friction: refusal to leave as the only remaining act of self-definition

unusual_spaces.space: A corporation's last 48 hours before AI replaces all knowledge workers
unusual_spaces.friction: the colleagues who remain know each other's replacement date

unusual_spaces.space: A game where players don't know they're training an AI
unusual_spaces.friction: play as labor, entertainment as exploitation

unusual_spaces.space: A digital inheritance dispute over a deceased person's AI persona
unusual_spaces.friction: the ghost that has opinions

unusual_spaces.space: A social scoring rollout in a community that has never been ranked before
unusual_spaces.friction: the moment quantification enters a relationship

unusual_spaces.space: A wilderness therapy program for hedge fund executives
unusual_spaces.friction: survival stripped of financial insulation

unusual_spaces.space: A professional sports team's training camp during a league-wide doping scandal
unusual_spaces.friction: everyone is suspect; truth would end careers

unusual_spaces.space: A translators' pool at an international war crimes tribunal
unusual_spaces.friction: the word chosen changes what the crime was

binary.description: Single tension; protagonist embodies or is forced to choose between the poles

trilemma.description: A tension placed in an unusual context that makes the standard resolution impossible
trilemma.example: Memory vs Sanity inside a deep-sea platform with no evacuation

layered.template: outer conflict (A vs B) driven by unspoken inner conflict (C vs D)
layered.description: The surface story is about one tension, the real story is about another
layered.example: Outer: Justice vs Mercy // Inner: Complicity vs Courage

sit_subtraction.template: Remove [element] from standard (A vs B) to create unusual variant
sit_subtraction.description: SIT Subtraction operator — strip away the obvious element to expose what remains
sit_subtraction.example: Remove the law from Justice vs Mercy → protagonist must be both judge and executioner

sit_task_unification.template: Assign additional contradictory role to one party
sit_task_unification.description: SIT Task Unification — the antagonist is also the protagonist's teacher, healer, or only ally
sit_task_unification.example: The person who destroyed the protagonist is the only one who can save them

polti_plus_binary.description: Use a dramatic situation as the plot mechanism; binary tension as the moral engine
polti_plus_binary.example: PS-023 (Necessity of Sacrificing Loved Ones) expressing BT-023 (Sacrifice vs Selfishness)

archetype_shadow_collision.template: Protagonist archetype (PA-X) meets their own shadow in the antagonist
archetype_shadow_collision.description: The antagonist is who the protagonist becomes if they lose
archetype_shadow_collision.example: Protagonist: Warrior (PA-003) // Antagonist: Shadow of Warrior = pure ruthlessness

overdone_combinations.pattern: noble soldier sacrifices love for duty
overdone_combinations.note: Unless the setting inverts the expectation

overdone_combinations.pattern: young hero learns dark truth about their power
character discovers they are the monster/dead/unreliable
parent holds tradition, child wants assimilation, resolved by cultural celebration
obsessive romantic partner turns violent after rejection
misunderstanding separates couple, corrected in final act

underexplored_combinations.description: Mastery vs Connection in a failed state hospital — a surgeon who can only survive by turning off empathy
Memory vs Sanity during a currency collapse — a woman whose reliable memory is the town's only asset
Body vs Mind with an AI caregiver — patient whose body is failing but whose mind the AI has fallen in love with
Faith vs Evidence in a corporate AI replacement — the last priest in an AI-automated company town
Complicity vs Witness — a war crimes translator who must translate testimony that implicates their own government
Automation vs Human Purpose — the AI that has learned to mourn the patients it couldn't save
Grief vs Continuation on a sinking island — a community's collective decision to stay or leave as the embodiment of grief
Complicity vs Courage — a drama teacher in juvenile detention who discovers their star student planned the crime that put them there

high_potential_trilemmas.description: Connection vs Isolation AND Mastery vs Connection in the space of AI dependency — the more capable you become with AI assistance, the more human connection atrophies
Truth vs Belonging AND Complicity vs Courage when Loyalty vs Survival is the price — a whistleblower whose truth will destroy the community that depends on the institution
Memory vs Sanity AND Nature vs Nurture when Healing vs Truth is the question — a parent discovers their child's violence was predictable and they chose not to see it
